questions.q: Who delivers the teaching of the Bhagavad Gita to Arjuna?
questions.explanation: Krishna, serving as Arjuna's charioteer, delivers the Gita on the battlefield.

questions.q: On the eve of which war is the Bhagavad Gita spoken?
questions.explanation: The dialogue takes place just before the eighteen-day Kurukshetra War begins.

questions.q: What role does Krishna play for Arjuna during the Kurukshetra War?
questions.explanation: Krishna vowed not to fight and instead became Arjuna's charioteer and counselor.

questions.q: How many chapters does the Bhagavad Gita contain?
questions.explanation: The Gita comprises eighteen chapters, mirroring the war's eighteen days and the epic's eighteen parvas.

questions.q: What crisis prompts Krishna to teach the Gita?
options: Arjuna refuses to fight his own kinsmen
The Pandava army begins to flee
questions.explanation: Seeing teachers and relatives arrayed for death, Arjuna collapses in grief and refuses to fight.

questions.q: In which language was the Bhagavad Gita composed?
questions.explanation: The Gita is a Sanskrit poem embedded within the Mahabharata.

questions.q: What does the title 'Bhagavad Gita' mean?
questions.explanation: Bhagavad Gita translates as 'the song of the Blessed Lord'.

questions.q: Roughly how many verses make up the Bhagavad Gita?
questions.explanation: The standard text runs to about 700 shlokas.

questions.q: Who asks the questions that drive the Gita's dialogue forward?
questions.explanation: The Gita unfolds as Krishna's answers to Arjuna's doubts and questions.

questions.q: Where do Krishna and Arjuna halt their chariot for the Gita discourse?
options: Atop a hill overlooking the field
At the banks of the Ganga
questions.explanation: Arjuna asks Krishna to draw the chariot between the opposing armies, where his despair begins.

questions.q: Who narrates the Bhagavad Gita to the blind king Dhritarashtra?
questions.explanation: Sanjaya, granted divine sight, reports the battlefield dialogue to Dhritarashtra.

questions.q: Who granted Sanjaya the divine vision that let him witness the Gita?
questions.explanation: Vyasa bestowed celestial sight on Sanjaya so he could see all that happened at Kurukshetra.

questions.q: In which parva of the Mahabharata is the Bhagavad Gita found?
questions.explanation: The Gita occupies chapters of the Bhishma Parva, the book of the war's first phase.

questions.q: What is the Vishvarupa that Krishna reveals to Arjuna?
options: A vision of the war's outcome
The form of Vishnu reclining on Shesha
questions.explanation: In chapter eleven Krishna displays his boundless cosmic form containing all worlds and beings.

questions.q: What does Krishna give Arjuna so he can behold the Vishvarupa?
questions.explanation: Krishna grants Arjuna divya chakshu, divine sight, since mortal eyes cannot see the cosmic form.

questions.q: Which teaching of the Gita urges action without attachment to results?
questions.explanation: Nishkama Karma, selfless action, is the heart of the Gita's Karma Yoga.

questions.q: In the famous verse 'yada yada hi dharmasya', what does Krishna promise?
options: To protect Arjuna in every battle
To end the age of Kali
To grant liberation to all warriors
questions.explanation: Krishna declares he manifests age after age to restore dharma whenever it decays.

questions.q: Krishna's first major teaching consoles Arjuna with which truth?
options: The soul is eternal and cannot be slain
questions.explanation: Krishna teaches that the atman neither kills nor dies; only bodies perish.

questions.q: Which yoga in the Gita is the path of loving devotion?
questions.explanation: Bhakti Yoga, devotion to the Lord, is exalted especially in the Gita's twelfth chapter.

questions.q: What had Arjuna done with his weapons at the depth of his despair?
options: Hurled them at the enemy lines
Cast aside his bow and sank down
questions.explanation: Overcome with grief, Arjuna dropped Gandiva and collapsed into the chariot seat.

questions.q: The Gita describes the 'sthitaprajna' — who is that?
options: A person of steady, unshakable wisdom
A sage who renounces all speech
questions.explanation: Chapter two describes the sthitaprajna, one whose insight stays steady amid joy and sorrow.

questions.q: By what name is Arjuna addressed as 'son of Pritha'?
questions.explanation: Partha derives from Pritha, another name of Arjuna's mother Kunti.

questions.q: Which chapter of the Gita is devoted to the vision of the cosmic form?
questions.explanation: Chapter eleven, the Vishvarupa Darshana Yoga, contains the revelation of the universal form.

questions.q: In the Vishvarupa vision, Krishna declares 'I am...' what, 'destroyer of worlds'?
questions.explanation: Krishna proclaims 'kalo'smi' — I am Time, the destroyer of worlds.

questions.q: What does the charama shloka (18.66) instruct Arjuna to do?
options: Meditate on the syllable Om at death
Abandon all dharmas and surrender to Krishna alone
questions.explanation: The final instruction is total surrender: Krishna promises to free Arjuna from all sin.

questions.q: Among the Pandavas, Krishna says in the Gita's tenth chapter that he is whom?
questions.explanation: Listing his glories, Krishna says that among the Pandavas he is Dhananjaya, Arjuna himself.

questions.q: Which tree does Krishna name as his manifestation among trees?
questions.explanation: In the Vibhuti Yoga, Krishna says that among trees he is the ashvattha.

questions.q: What are the three gunas analyzed at length in the Gita?
questions.explanation: The Gita's fourteenth chapter dissects sattva, rajas and tamas, the three strands of nature.

questions.q: Whose words open the Bhagavad Gita with the question 'dharmakshetre kurukshetre'?
questions.explanation: The Gita opens with Dhritarashtra asking Sanjaya what his sons and the Pandavas did on the field of dharma.

questions.q: On which day is Gita Jayanti, the Gita's traditional anniversary, observed?
questions.explanation: Tradition holds the Gita was spoken on the Mokshada Ekadashi of the month of Margashirsha.
